extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian attacked from breakaway on final climb with 30-second deficit, caught YATES Adam on descent hitting 50mph to win Stage 16 Pyrenees opener.
tactical_patterns: Alaphilippe deliberately conserved energy on final climb knowing 30-second deficit manageable given his superior descending ability versus breakaway companions.
Quick-Step's stage-hunting strategy produces 50+ wins by mid-season while maintaining both sprint support (Gaviria) and breakaway opportunism.
Yates attacked for stage redemption after poor GC Alps performance, showing GC failures make aggressive breakaway riding more acceptable tactically.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: descended at 50mph maintaining complete control, demonstrating calculated risk management by accepting 30s deficit on climb for descent advantage
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: crashed on descent left-hander at speed, remounted quickly but fear factor visibly elevated preventing effective chase
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: negative: defending KOM champion not riding same stratosphere as 2017, already 49 points behind Alaphilippe with limited recovery opportunities
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: earning Team Sky respect at young age but domestique duties preventing white jersey competition despite talent
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: facial expression suggested already projecting to tomorrow's steep stage rather than enjoying yellow jersey security
